mnemonics.title: CRITICAL SECTION REQUIREMENTS
mnemonics.content:
MUTUAL EXCLUSION: ONLY ONE AT A TIME (MUTEX)
PROGRESS: THOSE OUTSIDE CAN DECIDE (P)
BOUNDED WAITING: LIMITED RE-ENTRIES AFTER REQUEST (B)

SHORT: MPB - MUST PROTECT BUDDY'S CODE

acronyms.usage: Mechanisms for processes to communicate and synchronize, including shared memory and message passing
Code segment that accesses shared resources and requires mutual exclusion
Atomic hardware instruction used to implement locks without busy waiting

visualTips.visualization: Think of a single-door room (critical section). Only one person can enter at a time. A guard (lock) checks if room is empty before allowing entry. Others must wait outside (remainder section) until the room becomes available.

Imagine pressing a button that instantly changes a light from OFF to ON - you cannot observe it in a partially on state. It happens completely in one instant, like flipping a coin - no middle state exists.

analogies.analogy: Traffic at a single-lane bridge. Cars (processes) from both sides cannot cross simultaneously. Traffic lights (synchronization primitives) control access - only one direction can use the bridge at a time to prevent collisions.
A single toilet in an office. Only one person can use it at a time. The door lock (mutual exclusion) ensures privacy and prevents awkward situations. Others must wait their turn.

At a concert with limited entry, after someone requests to enter, only a limited number of others can enter before the first requester gets their turn. This prevents starvation - everyone eventually gets served.
